title: Tresk Im'nel

'''Tresk Im'nel''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]], who joined the [[Bothan Diplomatic Corps]] when [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|captured]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. He became part of the [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] staff of [[Senator/Legends|Counselor]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]], a preeminent Bothan, but grew concerned over the appropriateness of Bothan politicking to the fledgling Republic. This led him to seek a new role: he attached himself to a diplomatic team that was persuading old [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Planet/Legends|planets]] to join the Republic.

Im'nel later realized that his effectiveness as a diplomat was due, in part, to latent [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]]. Cognizant of the importance of restoring the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Im'nel reluctantly left his diplomatic post, and began [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] at the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. As a member of the [[New Jedi Order]], he then worked independently for ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]], before helping defend the galaxy from the rampant [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. Im'nel was one of Jedi survivors of this [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]].

==Biography==
===Diplomat===
Tresk Im'nel was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]],<ref name="wotc" /> a member of the [[Im family]] and [[Clan Onel]].<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> He was alive during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. When the Alliance [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|wrested]] control of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]], from the Empire, Im'nel joined the [[Bothan Diplomatic Corps]]. He journeyed to Coruscant to join the [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] staff of [[Senator/Legends|Counselor]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]], a preeminent Bothan politician; however, Im'nel soon grew disillusioned with the ambitious [[Council]]or's methods. While recognizing that Fey'lya's acts were merely typical Bothan politics, he nevertheless worried that the fledgling [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was not robust enough to withstand such manipulative tactics. In response to this, Im'nel arranged to join a diplomatic team that focused on bringing former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] into the New Republic fold. In his mind, this was work he could be proud of, helping to building a stronger Republic that could withstand internal strife.<ref name="wotc" />

Im'nel was incredibly successful at his diplomatic endeavors; he possessed an ability to intuitively address [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassadors]]' fears and desires. A product of many years of training, Im'nel at first thought nothing of his special skill. However, over time, he realized it went beyond the ordinary; at a meeting with Councilor and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], the Bothan's suspicions were confirmed: his skills were due to latent [[The Force/Legends|Force]] talents. Although reluctant to leave his work in the [[New Republic Diplomatic Corps/Legends|Diplomatic Corps]], Im'nel recognized the importance of restoring the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Leaving his career as a diplomat, Im'nel promptly journeyed to the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to receive training from [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] academy.<ref name="wotc" />

===Jedi Knight===
After graduating from the Academy as a [[Jedi Consular]], Im'nel worked for ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] as a solo operative, carrying out the work of the [[New Jedi Order]]. The contacts he'd forged during his time as a diplomat enabled him to call in many favors during his work. Im'nel ceased his independent work when the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaded]] from beyond the galaxy, choosing to work alongside other [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to help protect the Jedi Order and the New Republic.<ref name="wotc" /> Although many Jedi fell during the long war, Im'nel was one of the survivors, joining many others at the [[Conclave on Zonama Sekot]] at the conclusion of the war.<ref name="The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
A member of the Bothan Diplomatic Corps, Im'nel was a principled Bothan who was disillusioned by the Bothan political methods employed by Borsk Fey'lya. He sought more fulfilling work, and so Im'nel arranged to join a diplomatic team focusing on persuading Imperial worlds to join the New Republic. He was very successful in his diplomatic endeavors, possessing an ability to intuitively address ambassadors' fears and desires that was later discovered to be the product of his Force-sensitivity. After becoming a Jedi Knight, Im'nel used his skill as a diplomat to aid his work as a Jedi.<ref name="wotc" />

Im'nel could read and write [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], and was an eloquent speaker, able to inspire confidence in those around him. He also had some proficiency in forgery and [[Computer/Legends|computer]] use.<ref name="wotc" />

==Powers and abilities==
Im'nel was a Force-sensitive, and had his affinity with the Force affirmed by Organa Solo. After received Jedi training, Im'nel became proficient in several [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]], including the ability to [[Force sense/Legends|enhance his senses]] and to communicate [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]]. Im'nel also received vague impressions of the presence of the Force, including emanations of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. The strength of these impressions was relative to the strength of the Force emanation.<ref name="wotc" />

==Equipment==
Throughout his career as both a diplomat and a Jedi, Im'nel owned a second-hand [[Tresk Im'nel's translator droid|translator droid]]. He also commonly carried with him a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], multi-channel [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], [[Code cylinder/Legends|code cylinder]] and [[Credit chip/Legends|credit chip]]. He also carried a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] as a side-arm, which was later complimented with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="wotc" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[J.D. Wiker]] created Tresk Im'nel for the article, "Tresk Im'nel, Bothan Noble," published on the [[Wizards of the Coast]] website in [[2002]]. In addition to a short biography, this article provided [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] statistics showing Im'nel's progression from a beginner Noble to a Jedi Consular. In [[2003]], the component parts of Im'nel's name were included in the ''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]'', which explained the structure of Bothan nomenclature. That year, author [[James Luceno]] mentioned Im'nel in ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'', the final book of the [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series, as part of a list of surviving Jedi.

==Appearances==
*''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' {{1st}}
